prologue: ^When I was fifteen years old, half of my village died from a disease no one could name.
^And more kept dying, day after day.
^I became a doctor and spent many years searching for a cure.
^But the last ingredients — the Moonleaf, the Silverroot — they have been extinct for two hundred years.
^The village witch found me. She said she could send me back.
^Five days. That is all she could give me.
time_transition: ^Five days. Two herbs. I need to move fast.
^The village with the Shimmerleaf seems closer. I'll start there.
day1_arrival: ^Someone here must know about the Shimmerleaf.
village_hub: ^I have learned enough. Time to head out.

b: ^You are not from here. What do you want?
^My name is Aiden. I am looking for the Shimmerleaf.
^Shimmerleaf. Long way to come for a plant.
^It is important. Do you know it?
^You will need to camp in the forest. Ask me what you need.

mara_questions: ^What do you know about the Shimmerleaf?
^Have you seen anyone else pass through?
mara_campsite: ^Find somewhere high — not a hilltop, just higher than what surrounds it. Water runs downhill. Sleep in a low spot and you wake up wet. Or you do not wake up at all.
^Stay back from water. Fifteen steps at least. The bank looks stable until it is not.
^Tell me — flat ground right beside a stream, or a slight rise thirty steps away. Which do you take?
^The flat spot — easier access to water

c-0: ^Easy access to water. Easy access to flooding. The rise. Always the rise.
c-1: ^Good. You are thinking like someone who wants to wake up dry.

mara_shimmerleaf: ^Grows near water but not in it. Look for damp ground on a slope — where runoff collects but does not flood.
^The north-facing edges of the forest, mostly. Less sun. The plant likes the shade.
^It only blooms after rain. You will not find it on a dry night.
mara_others: ^Two, last week. One camped by the river bend. Gone before sunrise — tent half-buried in mud by morning.
^The other listened. She is probably doing fine out there.
^Some people learn from others. Some learn from the mud.

b: ^You look lost. First time out here?
^Then you will need to survive in the forest. Ask away.

finn_questions: ^Any dangers I should watch for out there?
finn_overhead: ^The trees will kill you before the cold does — trust me. Dead branches. Widowmakers, we call them. Always look up before you pitch your tent. Always.
^And never build fire under a canopy. One spark into dry leaves overhead and it all comes down on you. Open sky above your fire. No exceptions.
^Two spots. One under a big oak — sheltered. One in a clearing — open sky but you would feel the wind. Which one?

c-0: ^That oak has three dead branches I can see from here. Any one comes down in the night... Clearing. Always the clearing.
c-1: ^Exactly. Wind you can deal with. A branch through your tent you cannot.

b: ^You have the look of someone who has come a long way.
^I have. I am looking for the Shimmerleaf.
^Then sit for a moment. There are things worth knowing.

isla_questions: ^What do you know about making camp?
^What do you know about the Shimmerleaf?
isla_camp: ^Wind is the one people forget. They think about rain, they think about cold. Not wind — until it is three in the morning and their fire is ash and their blankets are wet.
^Face your shelter away from the wind. Find something solid at your back. A boulder, a bank, a hill.
^And cold air flows downhill just like water. Hollows fill up with cold before anywhere else. Sleep high. Sleep dry. Sleep warm.
^A hollow between two hills — sheltered, soft ground. Or a flat open space on the hillside — exposed, higher up. Where do you sleep?

c-0: ^Cold air pools in hollows like water in a bowl. By midnight shivering. By dawn sick. The hillside. Cold flows down — remember that.
c-1: ^You understand. Most people do not until they have spent a night in a hollow.

isla_shimmerleaf: ^My grandmother collected it. Said you could never go looking while the rain was falling.
^The rain wakes something in the leaf. Half an hour after a storm passes, they glow — pale green. Easy to spot. But only for a short while.
^You need to already be there when the rain stops. Camped and waiting.
^That is the only way. Be in the right place before the storm. Not after.
day1_end: ^Time to head out. The storm will not wait.
day2_transition: ^The forest is north. Half a day's walk.
^Isla said to be there before the storm hits. I need to move.
day2_forest: ^Rain is coming. I can feel it in the air.
^I need to make camp now — and be ready to search the moment it stops.
day2_campsite: ^Two spots. I need to decide before the rain hits.

b: ^Higher ground. If the rain pools anywhere, it will not be here.
^The rain started. The ground is already getting soft.
^It is going to be a cold night.

day2_fire_intro: ^The fire spot is clear. I need to get this lit and keep it alive through the night.
day2_fire_collect: ^I need to gather what I can find to start the fire.
day2_fire_campsite: ^Now to get this fire built and lit.

b: ^Strong fire. This will hold through the night.
^A small flame. It will have to do.
^Nothing. I could not get this started.
^The night is going to be brutal without fire.

day2_search_good: ^There — at the edge of the tree line. A pale green glow.

b: ^Shimmerleaf. The rain woke it up. And I was here to see it.
^I was too slow. The glow faded before I could reach it.

day2_search_poor: ^The rain stopped. But this ground — I can barely move without sinking.
^The window is open. I cannot waste it.

b: ^I can see something — at the edge of the light.
^Got it. But this ground nearly cost me everything.
^I could see the glow — just out of reach. The mud held me back.

^Found it.

^Isla was right. Be there before the storm. Not after.

^Found it. But I chose wrong last night.

^Higher ground. I will not forget that again.
^The fire never started. I could not see anything out there.
^The fire went out. I spent the night shivering. I could not find anything in the dark.
^I was there. The window was open. But I was too slow.
^I pushed too hard. My body gave out before I could finish.

day2_buffer: ^One more night. I have to get it right.
day2_buffer_mara: ^You are back. I can see it on your face.

c-0: ^Higher ground. Always. The water runs away from you, not toward you.
c-1: ^Heavy wood is wet inside even when it looks dry. Go by weight. And keep the fire pit off damp ground.

day2_buffer_leave: ^One more night. Same forest, same herb.
^This time I know what I am walking into.
worst_ending: ^I have run out of time. The window is closing.
day3_transition: ^Now I need to find the next herb before the window closes.
